info.title: Talkover API Documentation
info.description:
API documentation for Talkover - AI-powered voice conversation platform.

This OpenAPI document covers a curated subset of the API. The full reference (including Calendars, SIP Trunks, Auto Topup, Callback Agents, Conversation Flow, Campaign Webhooks, Campaign SFTP, Phone Number release flow, Action Integrations, Account API and more) is available as guided pages under **API Reference**.

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Voice agent CRUD, partial updates, publishing, pricing and advanced settings.

tags.name: Training
tags.description: Knowledge entries attached to an agent.

tags.name: Actions
tags.description: Webhook, transfer, hold and integration actions executed by the agent during a call.

tags.name: Action Integrations
tags.description: Catalog of pre-built integrations available to attach to actions.

tags.name: Conversation Flow
tags.description: Node-graph conversation flow configuration for flow-driven agents.

tags.name: Calls
tags.description: Make calls, list calls, end calls, find active calls.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Campaign CRUD, scheduling, retry and DNC rules.

tags.name: Campaign Webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks scoped to a campaign (lifecycle events).

tags.name: Campaign SFTP
tags.description: SFTP-based contact list import and result export.

tags.name: Callback Agents
tags.description: Inbound agents that handle return calls from outbound source agents.

tags.name: Calendars
tags.description: Calendars, members, events, attendees, availability rules, provider connections.

tags.name: Phone Numbers
tags.description: List numbers and submit release requests.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Agent webhook configuration, delivery logs, retry.

tags.name: Custom SIP Trunks
tags.description: Enterprise: register custom SIP trunks and DIDs.

tags.name: Account API
tags.description: User session endpoints: profile, personal access tokens, credits.

tags.name: Utility
tags.description: Health checks and other utility endpoints.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by agent name or label

initial_message.description: Initial message the agent will say

ask_if_human_present_on_idle.description: Whether to ask if human is present when idle

example.label: Customer Service Agent
example.name: Customer Service Agent
example.initial_message: Hello, how can I help you today?

curl.source:
curl -X POST \
  '{{baseUrl}}/agents' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
  -d '{
    "label": "Customer Service Agent",
    "name": "Customer Service Agent",
    "language": "en-US",
    "interrupt_sensitivity": "medium",
    "endpointing_sensitivity": "medium",
    "ivr_navigation_mode": "standard",
    "conversation_speed": 1.0,
    "initial_message_delay": 2,
    "ask_if_human_present_on_idle": true,
    "idle_time_seconds": 30,
    "llm_temperature": 0.7,
    "enable_recording": true,
    "direction": "inbound",
    "who_speaks_first": "agent"
  }'

php.source:
<?php

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();
$response = $client->post('{{baseUrl}}/agents', [
    'headers' => [
        'Authorization' => 'Bearer YOUR_TOKEN',
        'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
    ],
    'json' => [
        'label' => 'Customer Service Agent',
        'name' => 'Customer Service Agent',
        'language' => 'en-US',
        'interrupt_sensitivity' => 'medium',
        'endpointing_sensitivity' => 'medium',
        'ivr_navigation_mode' => 'standard',
        'conversation_speed' => 1.0,
        'initial_message_delay' => 2,
        'ask_if_human_present_on_idle' => true,
        'idle_time_seconds' => 30,
        'llm_temperature' => 0.7,
        'enable_recording' => true,
        'direction' => 'inbound',
        'who_speaks_first' => 'agent'
    ],
]);
$data = json_decode($response->getBody());

404.description: Agent or voice template not found

example.prompt: You are a helpful customer service agent...

curl.source:
curl -X PUT \
  '{{baseUrl}}/agents/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/knowledge' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
  -d '{
    "prompt": "You are a helpful customer service agent..."
  }'

php.source:
<?php

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();
$response = $client->put('{{baseUrl}}/agents/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/knowledge', [
    'headers' => [
        'Authorization' => 'Bearer YOUR_TOKEN',
        'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
    ],
    'json' => [
        'prompt' => 'You are a helpful customer service agent...'
    ],
]);
$data = json_decode($response->getBody());

php.source:
<?php

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();
$response = $client->post('{{baseUrl}}/agents/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/trainings', [
    'headers' => [
        'Authorization' => 'Bearer YOUR_TOKEN',
        'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
    ],
    'json' => [
        'instruction' => 'When a customer asks about refunds, explain the 30-day return policy...'
    ],
]);
$data = json_decode($response->getBody());

<?php

$client = new \GuzzleHttp\Client();
$response = $client->post('{{baseUrl}}/agents/123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000/actions', [
    'headers' => [
        'Authorization' => 'Bearer YOUR_TOKEN',
        'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
    ],
    'json' => [
        'name' => 'Transfer to Human',
        'type' => 'transfer',
        'trigger' => 'customer asks for human',
        'config' => [
            'phone_number' => '+1234567890'
        ]
    ],
]);
$data = json_decode($response->getBody());

php.source:
<?php

        'max_calls_per_day' => 150
    ],
]);
$data = json_decode($response->getBody());

payload.description: Replacement variables for the campaign agent prompt

context.description: Custom data to be sent back to client via webhooks

post.summary: Create external call or use existing telephony
post.description:
Create calls that can use either existing telephony flow or external websocket transport based on agent configuration. The request body structure determines the call type:
- Simple payload with only 'to' field: Uses existing telephony flow
- Full payload with 'sid', 'from', 'to', 'direction': Creates external call with websocket transport

to.description: Phone number to call (international format) - for existing telephony flow

payload.description: Replacement variables for the agent prompt

context.description: Custom data to be sent back to client via webhooks

sid.description: Session ID for the external call. This should be provided by your external telephony provider.

from.description: The phone number making the call. Must be in international format (e.g., +1234567890).

to.description: The phone number to call. Must be in international format (e.g., +1234567890).

direction.description: Call direction. Typically "outbound" for outgoing calls.

payload.description: Replacement variables for the agent prompt.

context.description: Custom data to be sent back to client via webhooks

existing_telephony.description: Uses existing telephony flow when agent.use_transport = true

external_call.description: Creates external call when agent.use_transport = false

existing_telephony.description: Response when using existing telephony flow

demo_url.description: URL to the demo audio file

curl.source:
curl -X POST \
  '{{baseUrl}}/agents/9fbef0b7-8d4e-4a08-9207-66c22155721d/webhooks' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "name": "My Webhook",
    "webhook_url": "https://example.com/webhook",
    "events": ["event_phone_call_started", "event_phone_call_ended"]
  }'

curl -X PUT \
  '{{baseUrl}}/agents/9fbef0b7-8d4e-4a08-9207-66c22155721d/webhooks/9fcafbf2-7593-44d6-8cd7-1b221beba62a' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "name": "Updated Webhook",
    "enabled": false
  }'

use_transport.description: Whether to use existing telephony transport (true) or external websocket transport (false)

example.label: Customer Service Agent
example.name: Customer Service Agent
example.initial_message: Hello, how can I help you today?

websocket_url.description: WebSocket URL for external telephony provider (automatically routed to optimal regional endpoint)

WebSocketEvent.description: WebSocket event message structure for external telephony
